steps.title: Set up the percentage increase formula
steps.content:
When $f(x)$ increases by $e\%$ due to a unit increase in $x$, the percent increase is:
$$\frac{f(x+1) - f(x)}{f(x)} \times 100 = e$$

steps.title: Substitute and simplify the expressions
steps.content:
For the exponential function $f(x) = 15b^x$:
$$f(x+1) = 15b^{x+1} = 15b \cdot b^x = b \cdot f(x)$$
Substituting into the formula:
$$\frac{b \cdot f(x) - f(x)}{f(x)} \times 100 = e$$
$$\frac{f(x)(b - 1)}{f(x)} \times 100 = e$$
$$(b - 1) \times 100 = e$$

steps.title: Identify the answer choice
steps.content: Rearranging the result: $e = 100(b - 1)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
